topic_or_source: Argentina came from behind to beat England 2-1 in the World Cup semifinal, reaching the final against Spain after scoring twice late in the match.

paragraphs: Argentina will play Spain in the World Cup final after a dramatic 2-1 victory over England in the semifinal. The match took place on July 15, 2026. Argentina had to come from behind to win.

England scored first through Anthony Gordon. Argentina then found an equalizer through Enzo Fernandez. According to Reuters, Lionel Messi provided the assist for Fernandez's goal. That pass brought Argentina level and gave the team a new chance in the match.

The deciding moment came late in the game. Lautaro Martinez scored Argentina's winning goal in the 92nd minute. Reuters said Messi also sent the cross that led to the winning goal. Argentina's two late goals ended England's hopes and sent Argentina into the final.
The final score was Argentina 2, England 1. It was a close semifinal, with the result changing after England's opening goal. Argentina now moves on to face Spain.

subsections.heading: A Late Comeback
paragraphs: Argentina's win was a comeback. England took the lead when Anthony Gordon scored the opening goal. At that stage, Argentina needed at least one goal to return to the match.
Enzo Fernandez scored the equalizer for Argentina. Later, Lautaro Martinez scored in the 92nd minute. Those two goals changed a 1-0 deficit into a 2-1 victory.
The 92nd minute is very late in a football match. Martinez's goal was the final turning point in this semifinal.

subsections.heading: Messi's Important Role
paragraphs: Lionel Messi did not score in this semifinal, but Reuters reported that he helped create both Argentina goals. He provided the assist for Fernandez's equalizer.
Messi also sent the cross that led to Martinez's winning goal. A cross is a pass played into an area near the goal, usually from the side of the field.
His two key passes were connected to Argentina's path to the final against Spain.

sections.heading: In One Line
paragraphs: Argentina came from behind to beat England 2-1 and reach the World Cup final against Spain.
Fernandez equalized, and Martinez won the match in the 92nd minute.
Messi assisted the equalizer and supplied the cross for the winner, according to Reuters.

chunks:
Argentina's Late Semifinal Win

Argentina will play Spain in the World Cup final after a dramatic 2-1 victory over England in the semifinal. The match took place on July 15, 2026. Argentina had to come from behind to win.

England scored first through Anthony Gordon. Argentina then found an equalizer through Enzo Fernandez. According to Reuters, Lionel Messi provided the assist for Fernandez's goal. That pass brought Argentina level and gave the team a new chance in the match.

The deciding moment came late in the game. Lautaro Martinez scored Argentina's winning goal in the 92nd minute. Reuters said Messi also sent the cross that led to the winning goal. Argentina's two late goals ended England's hopes and sent Argentina into the final.

The final score was Argentina 2, England 1. It was a close semifinal, with the result changing after England's opening goal. Argentina now moves on to face Spain.

Today's Semifinal Turning Point Points

Point One

A Late Comeback

Argentina's win was a comeback. England took the lead when Anthony Gordon scored the opening goal. At that stage, Argentina needed at least one goal to return to the match.

Enzo Fernandez scored the equalizer for Argentina. Later, Lautaro Martinez scored in the 92nd minute. Those two goals changed a 1-0 deficit into a 2-1 victory.

The 92nd minute is very late in a football match. Martinez's goal was the final turning point in this semifinal.

Point Two

Messi's Important Role

Lionel Messi did not score in this semifinal, but Reuters reported that he helped create both Argentina goals. He provided the assist for Fernandez's equalizer.

Messi also sent the cross that led to Martinez's winning goal. A cross is a pass played into an area near the goal, usually from the side of the field.

His two key passes were connected to Argentina's path to the final against Spain.

In One Line

Argentina came from behind to beat England 2-1 and reach the World Cup final against Spain.

Fernandez equalized, and Martinez won the match in the 92nd minute.

Messi assisted the equalizer and supplied the cross for the winner, according to Reuters.
